finding: recentHasMore = length<recentTotal can stay true forever when recentTotal drifts above the real count (cross-device delete of an unloaded row, or a boundary row dropped by dedup); the last-item effect then hammers /api/conversations with empty tail pages
resolution: fixed — end-detection now anchored on the SERVER page size (pageItems.length<limit) + a no-progress guard (a page that adds nothing new stops paging), so a drifted total can't cause a runaway. Covered by TEST-3b/3c.

finding: first-load loadRecentConversations failure clears recentLoading but never sets recentInitialized → widget stuck on the spinner forever (mount effect keyed on recentInitialized never re-fires); regression vs the old empty-fallback
resolution: fixed — catch sets recentError; the widget shows a retryable ErrorState when the list is empty. Covered by TEST-3d + the seeded-recent-convos-error gallery cell.

finding: catch resets loading flags but not recentInitialized; stuck loading skeleton with no retry until an unrelated sync/reconnect
resolution: fixed — same recentError + ErrorState + retry as above.

finding: deleting a loaded recent row decrements recentTotal but does not re-anchor recentPage, so the next loadMoreRecent fetches an offset one past the shifted boundary and permanently skips the boundary conversation
resolution: fixed — delete/bulkDelete/sync-delete now re-anchor recentPage=floor(length/limit) so the next loadMore re-fetches from a limit-aligned boundary and dedup recovers the row.

finding: cross-device delete of an unloaded row leaves recentTotal inflated → one redundant loadMore of an empty tail page (self-heals)
resolution: fixed as a side effect of the server-page-size end-detection (a short/empty page now sets recentHasMore=false immediately).

finding: selectedId does an O(loaded-rows) scan calling the conversationHref extension hook per row on EVERY render; the virtualizer re-renders per scroll frame → hundreds of hook calls per frame → scroll jank
resolution: fixed — selectedId is now useMemo'd on [recentConversations, location.pathname], so scroll-only re-renders skip the scan.

finding: the 'Loading more' wrapper sets role=status+aria-live AND wraps Spin whose inner Spinner is also role=status → nested live regions → duplicate/garbled SR announcements
resolution: fixed — removed the outer role=status/aria-live; the Spinner is the single live region.

finding: ROW_H hardcoded 34px used as both estimateSize and a rigid fixed li height/pitch → at large text zoom / OS font the content exceeds 34px and rows overlap/clip (WCAG 1.4.4)
resolution: fixed — switched to dynamic virt.measureElement (ref+data-index) and removed the forced height; rows are content-height and grow with zoom.

finding: virtualized rows forced to a 34px rigid pitch with no inter-row gap, whereas a real Menu row is content-height inside a flex gap-0.5 (2px) → 2px-taller, abutting pills
resolution: fixed — measured content-height rows + pb-0.5 reproduce the 2px Menu inter-row gap.

finding: 'Loading more' renders a bare Spin with no visible caption; sighted users see only a spinning icon
resolution: fixed — Spin now has description='Loading more…' (visible caption).

finding: TEST-11's 'domRowCount<30' is vacuous — on first mount only ~20 rows are loaded so a non-virtualized list also passes; and it's layout-fragile (a tall viewport auto-loading page 2 + overscan can exceed 30 and flake)
resolution: fixed (proactively) — TEST-11 now scrolls all 45 rows in and asserts the NEWEST (top) row is UNMOUNTED (count 0) + DOM<45 — the decisive windowing proof.

finding: on a very tall sidebar viewport the mount-time auto-load chains successive page fetches until the window fills, pulling several pages before any user scroll
resolution: working-as-intended — a virtualized list MUST load enough rows to fill its viewport; the number of eager pages is bounded by viewportHeight/ROW_H, not unbounded. Matches every virtualized surface in the app (kit/table.tsx).

finding: rows beyond the virtual window aren't in the DOM and the OS viewport isn't keyboard-scrollable, so a keyboard-only/SR user can Tab to only the ~rendered buttons and can't reach the rest (WCAG 2.1.1)
resolution: inherent to DOM virtualization and shared by every virtualized surface in the app (kit/table.tsx VirtualTable). It is a STRICT IMPROVEMENT over the prior hard 20-row slice (which made row 21+ unreachable by ANY input), and the permission-gated /chats page is the full, searchable, non-virtualized WCAG-compliant alternative for reaching every conversation. Not regressed by this change; disproportionate to solve here.

finding: the row label uses text-start while the original Menu label span uses text-left; in an RTL locale the recent-chat titles align opposite to the Navigation/Tools menu labels
resolution: text-start is MANDATED by the lint:logical-direction guardrail for module code (text-left fails it) and is the more-correct RTL choice; the Menu's text-left survives only because kit files are lint-exempt. Aligning the kit Menu to text-start too is out of scope for this feature.

finding: menuRowClasses extraction verified byte-identical to the original inline Items classes (row/button/actions) — the Navigation/Tools menus render unchanged
resolution: no change needed — confirmed correct by the blind patterns reviewer via git show main:menu.tsx diff.

finding: MenuRowButton is a genuine shared kit primitive reused by both Items-consumers and the sidebar; no parallel re-implementation
resolution: no change needed.

finding: no Zustand reactive proxy read inside the virtual-items .map(); rows read from the local recentConversations array
resolution: no change needed.

finding: in-flight guards on recentLoading/recentLoadingMore prevent overlapping loads; no race found
resolution: no change needed.

finding: every recent-fetch entrypoint gates on Permissions.ConversationsRead (matches the endpoint read perm); owner-scoped server; no cross-user path
resolution: no change needed — covered by TEST-1b.

finding: row click nav, floating-dropdown bail guard, aria-current selection, hover-kebab/delete all preserved under virtualization
resolution: no change needed — covered by TEST-10.

finding: hardcoded English strings consistent with the pre-existing widget + sibling menus (repo has no i18n framework)
resolution: no change needed.

finding: header/rows fit the sidebar container; no duplicated headers or misplaced actions
resolution: no change needed.

finding: [re-audit R2] syncRecentFront merge-prepends up to `limit` new rows but did NOT re-anchor recentPage (unlike delete paths); once accumulated prepends reach `limit`, the next loadMoreRecent(recentPage+1) fetches a fully-overlapping page -> added===0 -> noProgress -> recentHasMore wrongly false, stranding older pages until a page-1 replace
resolution: fixed — syncRecentFront now sets recentPage=Math.floor(length/limit), mirroring the delete re-anchor. Covered by TEST-5c.

finding: [re-audit R2] the auto-load effect had no failure gate: on a persistent load-MORE failure recentHasMore stays true and the store only toggles recentLoadingMore, so when it flips false the effect re-fires immediately -> tight infinite loadMoreRecent loop hammering the API with no backoff until the network recovers or the user scrolls up
resolution: fixed — the effect now also gates on !recentError (a failed loadMore sets recentError), stopping the loop; scrolling away from the bottom clears it via clearRecentError so returning retries once. Covered by TEST-13.

finding: [re-audit R2b] deleting ALL loaded rows while more exist server-side strands the sidebar: recentConversations=[] -> Empty render has no virtual rows -> the auto-load effect (needs lastIndex>=0) never fires, so 'no conversations' shows though rows remain server-side, until an unrelated create/reconnect
resolution: fixed — delete/bulkDelete/sync-delete call refillRecentIfEmptied() which reloads page 1 when the loaded list drained to empty while recentHasMore. Covered by TEST-14/TEST-14b.

finding: [re-audit R2b] a load-more failure permanently strands paging when the loaded page fits the viewport: atEnd is always true (no scrollback), so the scroll-away clearRecentError could never run, and there was no visible load-more error/retry -> silently stuck at 20 rows
resolution: fixed — replaced the fragile scroll-to-clear recovery with an explicit visible 'Couldn't load more · Retry' affordance (chat-recent-loadmore-error/-retry) that clears the error and re-fires loadMoreRecent; the auto-load effect now only gates on !recentError. Covered by TEST-13 (retry leg).

finding: [re-audit R2b] aria-setsize derived from the (drift-prone) recentTotal announces a wrong 'X of N' to screen readers after cross-device churn
resolution: fixed — setSize = recentHasMore ? max(recentTotal,length) : length, so a fully-loaded list reports the exact rendered count and never under-reports.

finding: [re-audit R3] refillRecentIfEmptied's in-flight guard made it a no-op when a loadMore was already fetching: a delete emptying the list while a page-N loadMore is in flight would let the stale page-N resolve and append onto the emptied list (seen set empty), showing a mid-list-only view with the most-recent rows missing until the next sync
resolution: fixed — added a recentLoadSeq epoch: loadRecentConversations captures it and discards its result if it changed mid-flight; refillRecentIfEmptied bumps the epoch + clears the in-flight flags before reloading page 1, so the stale loadMore is dropped. Covered by TEST-14c.

finding: [re-audit R4] the conversation.created handler prepends + bumps recentTotal but was the ONE front-mutating path that omitted the recentPage re-anchor (syncRecentFront/deleteConversation/bulkDelete/sync-delete all apply it). Creating >=limit chats locally leaves recentPage=1 while the list grows, so the next loadMoreRecent fetches a fully-overlapping page -> noProgress -> older pages stranded
resolution: fixed — the created handler now re-anchors recentPage=floor(length/limit) on a genuine new prepend, matching the other paths. Covered by TEST-5 (added recentPage assertion).
